pred_reason: The labels SWC-101 (Integer Overflow and Underflow) and SWC-107 (Reentrancy) are reasonable because the contract has unprotected arithmetic operations vulnerable to overflows and a transferFrom function violating CEI pattern. Evidence: Transfer functions lack overflow protection (SWC-101); transferFrom updates recipient balance before sender (SWC-107); Uses Solidity 0.5.0 without built-in overflow checks

findings.Description: The contract lacks overflow/underflow protection in arithmetic operations (transfer and transferFrom functions), particularly dangerous as it uses Solidity 0.5.0 which doesn't have built-in protection. Found in both smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential token minting/destruction or fund theft through balance manipulation.

findings.Description: Critical functions lack proper access restrictions and administrative controls. Found in smart contract code only. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: No ability to pause contracts, freeze funds, or manage administrative functions.

findings.Description: transferFrom function violates CEI pattern by updating recipient balance before sender balance and allowance. Found in smart contract code only. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Could lead to reentrancy attacks if contract is extended with callback functionality.

findings.Description: Constructor is payable but provides no withdrawal mechanism, locking any ETH sent during deployment. Found in both smart contract code and static analysis results. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: ETH sent to contract during deployment becomes permanently inaccessible.

findings.Description: Constructor accepts arbitrary initialSupply without validation. Found in smart contract code only. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential deployment with zero or unrealistic supply values.

findings.Description: Interface-implementing functions use public visibility instead of external. Found in smart contract code only. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Slightly increased gas costs without security implications.

findings.Description: Constructor parameters shadow contract methods. Found in static analysis results only. This issue appears in the auditing result.
findings.Impact: Potential confusion but no direct security impact.
